title: Mausalaparvan - Canto 001
translation_text:
Janamejaya said: How were the Andhakas destroyed, O Lord, along with the Vrishnis? And the Bhoja maharathas, while Vasudeva looked on.

Vaishampayana said: Then, in the thirty-sixth year, there was a great quarrel among the Vrishnis. Driven by destiny, they struck each other with maces.

Janamejaya said: By whom were those heroes, the Vrishnis and Andhakas, cursed, that they perished? And the Bhojas also, O best of Brahmanas, tell me in detail.

Vaishampayana said: They saw Vishvamitra, Kanva, and Narada, the wealthy in austerities, and the heroes led by Sarana, who had come to Dvaraka.

Having adorned Sambhu like a woman, they said to him, "We are oppressed by the punishment of fate."

This woman is the wife of Babhru, who is desirous of a son and who has immeasurable energy. O sages, know well what she will give birth to.

Thus addressed, O king, they, being deceived and oppressed, replied to them. Listen to that, O king.

For the destruction of the Vrishnis and Andhakas, he will give birth to a terrible iron mace. This Sambha, the son of Vasudeva, will give birth to it.

By whom you have become very wicked, cruel, and full of anger, And destroyers of the entire family, except for Rama and Janardana.

The glorious Halāyudha will go to the ocean, having abandoned his body. Old age will pierce the great-souled Krishna lying on the ground.

Thus they spoke, O king, when they were deceived by those wicked ones. The sages, their eyes red with anger, then looked at each other.

Having spoken thus, those sages then approached Keshava.

Then the slayer of Madhu spoke to the Vrishnis, having heard this, "The knower of the end, the wise one, said to them, ""It must be so."""

Having said this, Hrishikesha again entered the house. The Lord of the universe did not wish to alter the course of death.

Then, the next day, Sāmba gave birth to that club, which was like a servant, for the destruction of the Vṛṣṇis and Andhakas.

They reported to the king the terrible curse that had been born. The king, with a distressed countenance, had it ground into fine powder.

By the king's command, the men threw it into the ocean. And in the city, they proclaimed by the order of Aśoka:

From now on, in all the houses of the Vrishnis and Andhakas here, The drinking of liquor should not be done by anyone living in the city.

And he who would do anything unknown to us, or drink anything anywhere, Living, he would mount the stake himself, along with his kinsmen.

Then, out of fear of the king, they all made a rule. The people, having understood the command of that great king,
shlokas_raw:
जनमेजय उवाचकथं विनष्टा भगवन्नन्धका वृष्णिभिः सहपश्यतो वासुदेवस्य भोजाश्चैव महारथाः

वैशंपायन उवाचषट्त्रिंशेऽथ ततो वर्षे वृष्णीनामनयो महान्अन्योन्यं मुसलैस्ते तु निजघ्नुः कालचोदिताः

जनमेजय उवाचकेनानुशप्तास्ते वीराः क्षयं वृष्ण्यन्धका ययुःभोजाश्च द्विजवर्य त्वं विस्तरेण वदस्व मे

वैशंपायन उवाचविश्वामित्रं च कण्वं च नारदं च तपोधनम्सारणप्रमुखा वीरा ददृशुर्द्वारकागतान्

ते वै साम्बं पुरस्कृत्य भूषयित्वा स्त्रियं यथाअब्रुवन्नुपसंगम्य दैवदण्डनिपीडिताः

इयं स्त्री पुत्रकामस्य बभ्रोरमिततेजसःऋषयः साधु जानीत किमियं जनयिष्यति

इत्युक्तास्ते तदा राजन्विप्रलम्भप्रधर्षिताःप्रत्यब्रुवंस्तान्मुनयो यत्तच्छृणु नराधिप

वृष्ण्यन्धकविनाशाय मुसलं घोरमायसम्वासुदेवस्य दायादः साम्बोऽयं जनयिष्यति

येन यूयं सुदुर्वृत्ता नृशंसा जातमन्यवःउच्छेत्तारः कुलं कृत्स्नमृते रामजनार्दनौ

समुद्रं यास्यति श्रीमांस्त्यक्त्वा देहं हलायुधःजरा कृष्णं महात्मानं शयानं भुवि भेत्स्यति

इत्यब्रुवन्त ते राजन्प्रलब्धास्तैर्दुरात्मभिःमुनयः क्रोधरक्ताक्षाः समीक्ष्याथ परस्परम्

तथोक्त्वा मुनयस्ते तु ततः केशवमभ्ययुः

अथाब्रवीत्तदा वृष्णीञ्श्रुत्वैवं मधुसूदनःअन्तज्ञो मतिमांस्तस्य भवितव्यं तथेति तान्

एवमुक्त्वा हृषीकेशः प्रविवेश पुनर्गृहान्कृतान्तमन्यथा नैच्छत्कर्तुं स जगतः प्रभुः

श्वोभूतेऽथ ततः साम्बो मुसलं तदसूत वैवृष्ण्यन्धकविनाशाय किंकरप्रतिमं महत्

प्रसूतं शापजं घोरं तच्च राज्ञे न्यवेदयन्विषण्णरूपस्तद्राजा सूक्ष्मं चूर्णमकारयत्

प्राक्षिपन्सागरे तच्च पुरुषा राजशासनात्अघोषयंश्च नगरे वचनादाहुकस्य च

अद्य प्रभृति सर्वेषु वृष्ण्यन्धकगृहेष्विहसुरासवो न कर्तव्यः सर्वैर्नगरवासिभिः

यश्च नोऽविदितं कुर्यात्पेयं कश्चिन्नरः क्वचित्जीवन्स शूलमारोहेत्स्वयं कृत्वा सबान्धवः

ततो राजभयात्सर्वे नियमं चक्रिरे तदानराः शासनमाज्ञाय तस्य राज्ञो महात्मनः
